description: Adding an unbounded label such as user id or request id to a metric, exploding series count and degrading the metrics backend.
body: Failure mode: a metric is tagged with a high-cardinality dimension, so each distinct value creates a new time series and the metrics store balloons, slowing queries and risking an outage — often discovered mid-incident when dashboards stall. Detection signals: series count spikes after a deploy; metrics backend memory/cost climbs; queries time out. Do not put per-request or per-user identifiers in metric labels. Safer fallback: remove the unbounded label, keep only bounded dimensions, move per-request detail to logs/traces, and add a cardinality budget alert.
